test_cases.title: Search returns all tasks when no filters are active
preconditions: At least one task exists in the system
The user is authenticated and has access to the task search feature
steps: Send a search request with no filters applied (status, assignee, and due date range all unset)
test_cases.expected_result: The response contains a non-empty list of tasks, including all tasks the user has access to

test_cases.title: Search with a single status filter returns only tasks matching that status
preconditions: Tasks exist with at least two distinct status values (e.g., 'Open' and 'Closed')
The user is authenticated and has access to the task search feature
steps: Send a search request with status filter set to a single value (e.g., 'Open')
Inspect each returned task's status field
test_cases.expected_result: Every task in the result set has the status value 'Open'; tasks with other status values are absent

test_cases.title: Search with multiple status values returns tasks matching any of the specified statuses
preconditions: Tasks exist with at least three distinct status values (e.g., 'Open', 'In Progress', 'Closed')
The user is authenticated and has access to the task search feature
steps: Send a search request with the status filter set to two values (e.g., 'Open' and 'In Progress')
Inspect the status field of each returned task
test_cases.expected_result: Every returned task has a status of either 'Open' or 'In Progress'; tasks with status 'Closed' are absent

test_cases.title: Search with all three filters active returns only tasks matching ALL filters simultaneously
preconditions: Tasks exist that satisfy various combinations of status, assignee, and due date
At least one task exists that matches status='Open', assignee='user_A', and due date within '2024-06-01' to '2024-06-30'
At least one task exists that matches status='Open' and assignee='user_A' but falls outside the specified due date range
The user is authenticated and has access to the task search feature
steps: Send a search request with status filter set to 'Open', assignee filter set to 'user_A', and due date range set to start='2024-06-01' and end='2024-06-30'
Inspect the status, assignee, and due date fields of each returned task
test_cases.expected_result: Every returned task has status='Open', is assigned to 'user_A', and has a due date between 2024-06-01 and 2024-06-30 inclusive; tasks failing any single filter criterion are absent

test_cases.title: Search with filters that match no existing tasks returns an empty result set
preconditions: No task exists that matches the combination of the chosen filter values
The user is authenticated and has access to the task search feature
steps: Send a search request with an assignee filter set to a valid user who has no assigned tasks and a status filter set to 'Closed'
test_cases.expected_result: The response returns a valid, empty result set (e.g., an empty array) with a success status code, and no error is raised

test_cases.title: Search with an invalid due date range (start date after end date) is rejected
preconditions: The user is authenticated and has access to the task search feature
steps: Send a search request with due date range where start date is '2024-12-31' and end date is '2024-01-01'
test_cases.expected_result: The system returns an error response indicating the due date range is invalid; no task results are returned

assumptions: The due date range filter is inclusive of both the start date and end date boundaries.
Multiple status or assignee values within a single filter are combined with OR logic, while different filter types (status vs. assignee vs. due date) are combined with AND logic.
The system returns an HTTP success status code (e.g., 200) even when the result set is empty, distinguishing an empty result from an error condition.
A user must be authenticated to access the task search feature; unauthenticated access is outside the scope of this requirement.
Providing no value for a filter type (omitting it) means that filter is inactive and imposes no restriction on results.
notes: The requirement does not specify: (1) what constitutes valid status values, making it impossible to enumerate exhaustive status-specific tests; (2) whether the due date range is inclusive or exclusive at boundaries — boundary behavior should be clarified; (3) how partial filter inputs are handled (e.g., providing a start date but no end date); (4) pagination, sorting, or result size limits on the returned task list; (5) whether the search is scoped to tasks visible to the requesting user or all tasks in the system, which has authorization implications not covered here; (6) behavior when an assignee filter references a non-existent user ID. These ambiguities represent coverage gaps.
